overview.minides: Toyota's first EV in India — built on the Heartect-e platform shared with Maruti e Vitara, with Toyota's 'Urban Tech' hammerhead design, up to 543 km ARAI range, 150 kW DC fast charging, Level 2 ADAS, JBL audio, 8-year battery warranty, and Battery-as-a-Service (BaaS) ownership.
overview.description: The Toyota Urban Cruiser Ebella marks Toyota's entry into India's mass-market EV segment, launched on May 29, 2026. It is built on the Heartect-e platform shared with the Maruti Suzuki e Vitara, manufactured at Suzuki's Gujarat facility. Three variants are offered — E1 (49 kWh, ₹18.00L), E2 (61 kWh, ₹20.80L), and E3 (61 kWh, ₹23.60L) — with the E1 using a 144 PS motor and the E2/E3 using a stronger 174 PS unit. Range spans 440 km (E1) to 543 km (E3) on ARAI certification. Key features include Toyota's 'Urban Tech' hammerhead design, a 10.25-inch touchscreen with i-CONNECT connectivity, a 10.1-inch digital driver display, a JBL premium audio system on top trims, ventilated front seats, sliding and reclining rear seats, a panoramic sunroof, 12-colour ambient lighting, 360-degree camera, Level 2 ADAS (E3 only), 7 airbags, and 150 kW DC fast charging. Toyota backs the Ebella with an 8-year/2,00,000 km battery warranty, Battery-as-a-Service plans, a 60% guaranteed buyback scheme, and over 500 EV-ready service centres.
